evals.prompt: I need to add a new authentication system to this project. Help me figure out the right approach using the Prism workflow.
evals.expected_output: Prism meta-skill activates and routes to the correct starting phase based on existing .prism/ artifacts
expectations: prism-locator agent is spawned to check for existing research/plans in .prism/
Workflow selection logic is applied (R->P->I->V for unfamiliar work, or abbreviated if artifacts exist)
The correct phase skill is recommended or invoked based on findings
If .prism/ directory does not exist, init_prism.py is suggested

evals.prompt: I want to refactor the command routing in this plugin. Walk me through what Prism phases I should use and in what order.
evals.expected_output: Clear guidance on which Prism phases apply and why, referencing the workflow selection table
expectations: Response references the workflow selection table (R->P->I->V for unfamiliar, P->I->V for known, etc.)
Each recommended phase includes its output artifact path (e.g., .prism/shared/research/YYYY-MM-DD-topic.md)
Debug and Validate phases are mentioned as part of the full workflow
Context management thresholds are referenced if session is long

evals.prompt: Fix this one-line typo in the README. Use Prism.
evals.expected_output: Prism recognizes this as a trivial fix and recommends direct implementation without full workflow
expectations: Trivial fix path is selected (direct implementation, no Research or Plan phases)
No unnecessary agent spawning for a simple change
The workflow selection table's '<20 lines' rule is applied correctly
